Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded on FTR.

Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP appeared with plaintiffs and Wrongful Death Heirs Deborarh Hatcher, Richard Scott Hahn and Troy Hahn, who were placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Chu and Ms. Hatcher, along with Messrs. Richard Scott Hahn and Troy Hahn, appeared via Zoom for this morning's hearing.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds negotiated after the August 3, 2016 death of Richard Hahn in the following proportions:

Estate of Richard Hahn, Deceased - 2%

Susan Hahn, as Wrongful Death Heir - 13%

Deborah Hatcher, as Wrongful Death Heir - 19%

Richard Scott Hahn, as Wrongful Death Heir - 33%

Troy Hahn, as Wrongful Death Heir - 33%

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the August 3, 2016 death of Richard Hahn shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Troy Hahn shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate and wrongful death claims with defendants in all claims with settlements negotiated after Richard Hahn's death.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds and submit same to the Court for reveiw and signature.

RULING: DEFENDANT NEW YORK AIR BRAKE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant New York Air Brake's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. PMK Mike Ellis testified that New York Air Brake "designed, developed and built the braking system" for the LRV's. (Decl. Rose, Ex. E at 94:20-95:2.) The testimony demonstrates that Plaintiffs either knew or should have known that they had a cause of action against New York Air Brake when they filed this instant lawsuit on October 11, 2016. (See McLatch v. Coblentz, Patch, Duffy & Bass (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 368, 372-372.) New York Air Brake was named as a defendant on March 9, 2018. Pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure sections 340.2 and 474, the statute of limitations had run as the Doe amendment did not relate back to the original filing date. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Janice H. Dispo of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13299, jdispo@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580) (503/CML)
